text: In headless mode (`claude -p`), a conductor driver can launch the generated Workflow as a BACKGROUND task and then end its turn ('the run will notify me when it completes') — but ending the turn exits the -p process, killing the orphaned workflow. Observed live 2026-07-14 in an arena cell (uglify-corner-cases, opus-4-8[1m]/high): flawless Setup (DoD frozen, verify-reports.cjs grounded, per-report workers planned), then Workflow launched backgrounded, driver ended turn at ~17.5 min, process exited, wf/ branch left with ZERO commits, cell scored failed. Reproduced 2/2 on the same cell (attempts 1029s/1050s, both wf/ branches zero-commit); the same day five SHORT cells completed fine — the driver backgrounds the workflow precisely when it anticipates a LONG run, so the bug bites hardest on the hardest tasks. SKILL.md should pin the invocation: run the Workflow with run_in_background: false (or explicitly forbid ending the turn while the workflow runs — poll/wait until Finalize completes) whenever the session is non-interactive. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): SKILL.md (or the scaffold's driver instructions) contains an explicit run_in_background/do-not-end-turn directive for the Workflow invocation; today it says nothing, leaving the driver free to orphan the run.

Conductor loses to a RAW single session on wide, decomposable tasks because its generated plan runs independent work items as SEQUENTIAL phases. Measured head-to-head 2026-07-14 on arena task uglify-corner-cases (six INDEPENDENT compiler bugs, cellCapSec=3600): raw sonnet-5 one-shot RESOLVED 6/6 in ~53 min; opus-4-8[1m]/high + conductor TIMED OUT at 3603s with only 2/6 fixed — its driver designed Setup -> DoDBaseline -> Report1..Report6 -> DoDGate and paid worker spin-up + a ~5-min full-suite verification per phase, serially. The six reports were embarrassingly parallel (disjoint transforms, one shared file but separable hunks; a per-worker worktree already exists). SKILL.md / the scaffold should instruct: when the frozen DoD decomposes into independent criteria, fan the workers out concurrently (Workflow parallel()/pipeline over per-criterion workers, verify once at the gate), reserving sequential phases for genuinely dependent steps. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): generate a plan for a task whose DoD has N independent checkable criteria and assert the emitted loop runs their workers inside one parallel()/pipeline stage rather than N sequential phase() blocks; today the scaffold emits strictly sequential phases.
